channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Local congressman reacts to inspectors general dismissed by President Trump
item.description: In the flurry of Trump's executive orders covering immigration raids, federal firings, funding freezes and tariff negotiations, the dismissing of 17 inspector generals has gotten little notice, but according to San Diego Congressman Mike Levin, this is a bigger issue than most understand.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Senate was a battleground in 2023, new Republican minority leader emphasizes setting priorities
item.description: The Nevada Senate is ready to get down to business, and the end of the 2023 Legislature is still fresh in the minds of Democrats and Republicans gathered in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Legislature observes 'family day,' targets high price of eggs as session starts
item.description: State lawmakers convened in Carson City on Monday, beginning the 83rd Legislative Session with the legislative chambers filled with family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump agriculture nominee, Brooke Rollins, clears key Senate hurdle ahead of confirmation vote
item.description: Brooke Rollins, the former president and CEO of the America First Policy Institute, had earned broad support from farmers and growers' groups ahead of her Senate confirmation vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Speaker’s Lobby: A palpably unfair act
item.description: In football, safeguards exist against subjectively subversive, "palpably unfair" acts. The same can't be said for Washington – much to Trump critics' recent chagrin.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms fossil fuel CEO Chris Wright as energy secretary. He vows to 'unleash' US resources
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate on Monday confirmed fossil fuel executive Chris Wright to serve as energy secretary, a key post to promote President Donald Trump’s efforts to achieve U.S. “energy dominance” in the global market.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC mayor downplays economic concerns of Trump deportation plan
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There have anecdotal reports that immigration raids are driving undocumented New Yorkers into the shadows. New York City has more than 400,000 undocumented workers who help prop up the economy; however, Mayor Eric Adams said he was not worried they would stop working.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Crucial moderate GOP senator backs Tulsi Gabbard ahead of committee vote
item.description: A crucial committee senator said she would vote to advance Tulsi Gabbard to a floor vote in a statement on Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Association representing thousands of FBI agents appeals to Congress to protect their jobs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The association representing thousands of FBI agents urged congressional leaders Monday to protect the jobs of employees at risk of punishment or possibly termination over their participation in investigations into the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NJ bill looks to give veteran nonprofits a boost
item.description: UNION, N.J. (PIX11) - Almost any veteran non-profit organization would tell you that more money means more critical services they can provide to those who've served our country. New Jersey Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy is authoring a bill that would hope to give certain veteran nonprofits a hand.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Democratic state Sen. Mallory McMorrow preparing to enter US Senate race in Michigan, AP sources say
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan state Sen. Mallory McMorrow is preparing to enter the Democratic primary for an open U.S. Senate seat in the state, multiple people familiar with her plans confirmed on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Even those who have not worked directly in DEI roles but went to the training have been put on administrative leave.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Second judge prepares to block Trump’s federal grant freeze
item.description: A second federal judge signaled she will block the Trump administration from implementing an across-the-board pause on federal grants later Monday. U.S. District Judge Loren AliKhan at the conclusion of a 90-minute hearing in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is USAID? Explaining the US foreign aid agency and why Trump and Musk want to end it
item.description: In its first two weeks, President Donald Trump’s administration has made significant changes to the U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer And Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico, Canada after they pledge to boost border enforcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is holding off on his tariff threats against Mexico and Canada for 30 days after the two U.S. neighbors agreed to boost border security efforts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces pause on Mexico tariffs after striking deal
item.description: Trump revealed he negotiated assistance from Mexico to strengthen the United States' southern border.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Secretary of Defense Hegseth to visit Fort Bliss, southern border
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) -- U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth will visit Fort Bliss and the southern border on Monday, Feb. 3, Fort Bliss announced. Hegseth will visit Joint Task Force North, which is headquartered at Fort Bliss. This organization is part of U.S. Northern Command.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: As Ken Martin takes helm of Democratic National Committee, DFL faces opening at the top
item.description: Democrats from around the country picked Martin to lead them forward over the weekend. Minnesota Democrats will have a similar discussion next month.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trial starts for alleged ringleader of massive COVID food fraud
item.description: A federal jury was selected Monday in the second criminal trial to stem from a lengthy federal investigation into Feeding Our Future. The judge put security measures in place after an alleged attempted bribe of jurors in the first trial in the case.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Here are some goods in the crosshairs of Trump's tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an order to put tariffs on U.S. neighbors Canada and Mexico, as well as China, starting Tuesday. Canada and Mexico quickly announced retaliatory tariffs, while China said it would take “necessary countermeasures.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Demonstrators protest Trump’s ‘dystopian’ immigration policies, promise to fight back
item.description: Demonstrators rallied on Minneapolis’ East Lake Street — a vibrant corridor of Mexican, Somali and other immigrant-owned businesses — packing the area on a grey and cold winter afternoon. They carried signs reading “All are welcome here” and “Immigrants Make America Great.
item.dc:creator: Feven Gerezgiher, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis, Florida GOP tensions spill out into open
item.description: Tensions are spilling out into the open between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) and members of his own party in the Sunshine State, pointing to a new discordant era of GOP-led politics in a state that has trended red over the last few cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis threatens Veto amid heated showdown
item.description: State lawmakers voted to pass the TRUMP act, which aims to support the president on immigration enforcement. Now, all eyes are on Governor DeSantis who promises a veto.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s FCC chief opens investigation into NPR and PBS
item.description: Brendan Carr, the new head of the Federal Communications Commission under President Trump, says the public broadcasters are being investigated for allegedly running commercials.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Senator calls RFK Jr.’s position on race and vaccines dangerous
item.description: In one of the most tense exchanges in a heated confirmation hearing, Senator Angela Alsobrooks called out past comments RFK Jr. made suggesting a different vaccine schedule for Black people.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: FCC chair launches investigation into NPR, PBS
item.description: The chairman of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is launching an investigation into NPR and PBS over their alleged "airing of commercials.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Mexican border cities can expect higher unemployment, bankruptcies with Trump tariffs, Baja business leaders say
item.description: According to Vega Álvarez, consumers on both sides of the border will be the ones who will pay for the tariffs as "things will have to be overpriced.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Patel dodges, demurs as Democrats press him on controversial statements
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, wouldn’t acknowledge or sought to distance himself from a host of his past public statements as Democrats pressed him during his confirmation hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis signals veto of TRUMP Act, criticizing lawmakers' immigration bill
item.description: After a late night for lawmakers in Tallahassee, Governor Ron DeSantis says his veto pen is ready to reject the TRUMP Act.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
